slides.title: The Problem with Arrays 📉
slides.content:
## Arrays are Too Stiff

If you are reading users from a database, you don't know if there are 10 users or 10,000. 
A fixed-size Array is useless here.

To solve this, Go provides **Slices**.

quizData.question: Why are Slices usually preferred over Arrays in Go?

options.text: Because slices can dynamically grow and shrink

quizData.explanation: Slices acts as flexible windows over arrays, dynamically sizing per your application's needs.

slides.title: Leaving the Brackets Empty ✂️
slides.content: To create a Slice instead of an Array, you literally just leave the size `[ ]` empty!

quizData.question: Which of these perfectly declares a Slice of integers?
quizData.explanation: An empty bracket `[]` accurately establishes a dynamic slice compared to a hardcoded array `[5]`.
